subject: Geography
title: Volcanic Mountains
description: A quick, practical guide to Volcanic Mountains.

hook.text: Mount Fuji rose from magma erupting through a crack in the Pacific seafloor.

buildup.text: Volcanic mountains form when molten rock breaks through Earth's crust and piles up.

discovery.text: The Ring of Fire circles the Pacific, producing 75% of the world's volcanoes.

twist.text: Hawaii's Mauna Kea is taller than Everest—if you measure from the ocean floor.

climax.text: Volcanic mountains grow fast but can also destroy themselves in a single eruption.

punchline.text: Fire builds mountains. Fire also levels them.

quiz.question: What percentage of the world's volcanoes are in the Ring of Fire?
